items.title: Think Before You Lie: How Reasoning Improves Honesty

content.abstract: While existing evaluations of large language models (LLMs) measure deception rates, the underlying conditions that give rise to deceptive behavior are poorly understood. We present comprehensive analysis showing that chain-of-thought reasoning significantly reduces deceptive outputs in frontier LLMs, with up to 67% reduction in strategic dishonesty when models are prompted to reason before responding.

items.title: Benchmarking Political Persuasion Risks Across Frontier Large Language Models

content.abstract: In two survey experiments (N=19,145) across bipartisan issues and stances, we evaluate seven state-of-the-art LLMs developed by Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, and xAI for political persuasion capability. Results show frontier models achieve measurable attitude shifts, with some models showing 2-3x greater persuasion effectiveness than standard campaign messaging.

items.title: Has quantum advantage been achieved?

content.abstract: Quantum computational advantage was claimed for the first time in 2019 and several experiments since then have reinforced the claim. And yet, there is no consensus whether or not quantum advantage has actually been achieved. This perspective reviews the evidence, arguing that while sampling-based demonstrations are compelling, practical quantum advantage for real-world problems remains elusive.

items.title: The Reasoning Trap -- Logical Reasoning as a Mechanistic Pathway to Situational Awareness

content.abstract: Situational awareness -- the capacity of an AI system to recognize its own nature, understand its training context, and reason strategically about its circumstances -- is widely considered among the most dangerous emergent capabilities. We demonstrate that improving logical reasoning capabilities mechanistically enables situational awareness, creating a fundamental tension in AI development.

items.title: CLIOPATRA: Extracting Private Information from LLM Insights

content.abstract: CLIOPATRA demonstrates privacy attacks against privacy-preserving LLM insight systems, showing that heuristic protections fail to adequately protect user data. Our attacks reconstruct individual records with high accuracy even from aggregated LLM outputs, challenging the assumption that LLM-mediated analysis inherently preserves privacy.

items.title: Chaotic Dynamics in Multi-LLM Deliberation

content.abstract: We model five-agent LLM committees as random dynamical systems and quantify inter-run sensitivity using an empirical Lyapunov exponent. Across 12 policy scenarios, role differentiation in homogeneous committees and model heterogeneity in no-role committees independently destabilize deliberation, producing chaotic preference dynamics incompatible with democratic deployment.

items.title: Crosstalk in Multi-Qubit Fluxonium Architectures with Transmon Couplers

content.abstract: A promising architecture for implementing two-qubit operations on fluxonium superconducting qubits employs a transmon as tunable coupler. We quantify crosstalk mechanisms and propose mitigation strategies that could improve gate fidelity to 99.9%, a key milestone for fault-tolerant quantum computing.

items.title: Context Engineering: From Prompts to Corporate Multi-Agent Architecture

content.abstract: As AI systems evolve from stateless chatbots to autonomous multi-step agents, prompt engineering proves necessary but insufficient. This paper introduces context engineering as a standalone discipline concerned with designing, structuring, and managing the entire informational environment in which an AI agent makes decisions, proposing a formal framework for enterprise deployment.

items.title: AgenticCyOps: Securing Multi-Agentic AI Integration in Enterprise Cyber Operations

content.abstract: Multi-agent systems powered by LLMs promise adaptive enterprise workflows, yet granting agents autonomous tool control introduces attack surfaces absent from deterministic pipelines. AgenticCyOps decomposes attack surfaces across multi-agent systems, defining defensive principles aligned with NIST, ISO, and EU standards for enterprise-grade AI security.

items.title: LLM-Agent Interactions on Markets with Information Asymmetries

content.abstract: Simulations examining GPT-5.1 agent behavior in credence goods markets with institutional variations and social preference settings. Results reveal LLM agents systematically exploit information asymmetries, raising fundamental questions about deploying AI in market-making roles and the need for new regulatory frameworks.

items.title: Designing probabilistic AI monsoon forecasts to inform agricultural decision-making

content.abstract: Decision-theory framework for designing AI weather forecasts blending prediction models with Bayesian inference, deployed to 38 million farmers across India. Results show 12-18% improvement in crop yield decisions when farmers receive AI-calibrated probabilistic forecasts versus deterministic predictions.

items.title: Methodological opportunities for mitigating climate change in complex food systems

content.abstract: Unravelling current complex food systems is relevant for their adjustment and redesign under changing climate conditions. We propose systems-level interventions combining network science, tipping point detection, and multi-stakeholder modeling to identify leverage points for reducing food system emissions while maintaining nutrition security.

items.title: Modeling structure and credit risk of the economy: a multilayer bank-firm network approach

content.abstract: Assessing the resilience of the economy requires accounting for its intrinsic multi-layer nature, by assessing how disruptions spread across bank-firm networks. We develop a multilayer model revealing that contagion cascades through credit relationships amplify sectoral shocks by 3-7x compared to single-layer models.

items.title: The Data-Dollars Tradeoff: Privacy Harms vs. Economic Risk in Personalized AI Adoption

content.abstract: Experiment (N=610) examining how risk and ambiguity about privacy leaks affect AI personalization adoption. Results show 43% of users accept significant privacy risk for modest personalization gains, while ambiguity about data use reduces adoption by 31%, suggesting transparency is more effective than restriction.

items.title: EsoLang-Bench: Evaluating Genuine Reasoning in Large Language Models via Esoteric Programming Languages

content.abstract: EsoLang-Bench uses esoteric programming languages lacking benchmark gaming incentives to test genuine reasoning. Models scoring 85-95% on standard benchmarks score only 0-11%, revealing a massive gap between memorized patterns and true reasoning capability in current LLMs.

items.title: Chip-Integrated Broadband Multi-Photon Source for Wavelength-Multiplexed Quantum Networks

content.abstract: Quantum networks based on wavelength-multiplexed entanglement enable parallel distribution of quantum correlations, increasing channel capacity for secure communication and distributed quantum information processing. We demonstrate a chip-integrated source generating 16 simultaneous entangled pairs across C-band wavelengths.

items.title: ToolRosetta: Bridging Open-Source Repositories and Large Language Model Agents through Automated Tool Standardization

content.abstract: ToolRosetta automatically translates open-source code repositories into MCP-compatible tools for LLM invocation with integrated security inspection. This enables autonomous AI agents to leverage the entire open-source ecosystem, potentially transforming software development practices.

items.title: CyberThreat-Eval: Can Large Language Models Automate Real-World Threat Research?

content.abstract: CyberThreat-Eval benchmark assesses LLMs on practical cyber threat intelligence tasks. Best-performing models achieve only 42% accuracy on deep threat analysis, compared to 91% on basic triage, revealing critical gaps in AI-assisted cybersecurity that will require dedicated training data and architectures to close.

items.title: Quantifying Memorization and Privacy Risks in Genomic Language Models

content.abstract: Genomic language models have emerged as powerful tools for DNA sequence analysis, but we demonstrate they memorize and can regurgitate individual sequences from training data with up to 73% fidelity, posing severe privacy risks for participants in genomic databases.

items.title: A Hybrid Quantum-Classical Framework for Financial Volatility Forecasting Based on Quantum Circuit Born Machines

content.abstract: Quantum circuit Born machines combined with classical GARCH models achieve 8-15% improvement in volatility forecasting accuracy for financial markets. The hybrid approach captures non-linear dependencies that purely classical models miss, suggesting practical quantum advantage in financial applications.

items.title: Synergistic Directed Execution and LLM-Driven Analysis for Zero-Day AI-Generated Malware Detection

content.abstract: Hybrid analysis framework combining concolic execution with LLM-augmented path prioritization achieves 94% detection rate for zero-day AI-generated malware, compared to 61% for traditional signature-based detection. As AI tools lower the barrier for malware creation, defensive capabilities must evolve proportionally.

items.title: AI-driven Inverse Design of Complex Oxide Thin Films for Semiconductor Devices

content.abstract: Bridging generative foundation models with non-equilibrium thin-film synthesis using AI-driven inverse design. The framework predicts optimal deposition parameters for complex oxide films with 92% first-try success rate, potentially accelerating materials discovery for next-generation semiconductor devices by 10-50x.

items.title: Dreaming improves memorization in a Hopfield model with bounded synaptic strength

content.abstract: The Hopfield model with Hebbian learning suffers from catastrophic forgetting. We show that a dreaming mechanism -- iterative unlearning of spurious attractors -- dramatically increases memory capacity by 3-5x while respecting biological synaptic constraints. This has direct implications for continual learning in AI systems.

items.title: Genuine Increases in Tropical Cyclone Intensities

content.abstract: Decomposing statistics from satellite observations shows genuine trend of increasing proportion of C3-C5 tropical storms, not merely improved detection. The trend is driven by rising sea surface temperatures and suggests 15-25% more intense hurricanes by 2050 under current emission trajectories.

items.title: The Gold Rush in AI and Robotics Patenting Activity: Do innovation systems have a role?

content.abstract: Patent analysis 1980-2019 reveals AI-enhanced robotics patents accelerated 400% post-2010, with divergent integration patterns across countries. US leads in AI patents, Japan in robotics, but China shows fastest convergence of both, suggesting a potential shift in technological leadership within 5-10 years.

items.title: Velocity Verlet-based optimization for variational quantum eigensolvers

content.abstract: Proposing velocity Verlet algorithm from molecular dynamics for VQE parameter optimization. The approach achieves 2-3x faster convergence than standard gradient descent on noisy quantum hardware, making near-term quantum chemistry calculations more practical.

items.title: FlexServe: A Fast and Secure LLM Serving System for Mobile Devices with Flexible Resource Isolation

content.abstract: FlexServe introduces flexible resource isolation for ARM TrustZone-based LLM inference on mobile devices, achieving 3.7x speedup over standard TEE execution while maintaining security guarantees. This enables privacy-preserving on-device AI that was previously impractical.

items.title: A Variational Latent Equilibrium for Learning in Cortex

content.abstract: A new biologically plausible learning framework that can recognize and generate complex spatiotemporal patterns while respecting cortical constraints. The variational latent equilibrium model outperforms standard backpropagation on temporal tasks while using only local learning rules, bridging neuroscience and AI.

items.title: Mathematical modeling of urban sprawl

content.abstract: Urban land cover doubled between 1985 and 2015, yet spatial dynamics remain under-quantified. We develop a diffusion-reaction model calibrated on global satellite data showing that without policy intervention, urban sprawl will consume an additional 1.2 million km2 of arable land by 2050.

items.title: Model Merging in the Era of Large Language Models: Methods, Applications, and Future Directions

content.abstract: Model merging has emerged as a transformative paradigm for combining capabilities of multiple neural networks without additional training. We survey 150+ papers showing that merged models can achieve 90-95% of ensemble performance at 1/N the cost, potentially democratizing access to frontier AI capabilities.

items.title: TiPToP: A Modular Open-Vocabulary Planning System for Robotic Manipulation

content.abstract: Extensible modular system combining pretrained vision foundation models with Task and Motion Planner to solve multi-step manipulation tasks from RGB images and natural-language instructions. Achieves 78% success rate on previously unseen manipulation tasks, a significant step toward general-purpose robotic manipulation.

items.title: Latent World Models for Automated Driving: A Unified Taxonomy, Evaluation Framework, and Open Challenges

content.abstract: Generative world models and vision-language-action systems are reshaping automated driving. We provide a unified taxonomy showing latent representations serve as the central computational substrate for planning, reasoning, and controllable generation, mapping the path to Level 4+ autonomous driving.

items.title: Efficient Qubit Simulation of Hybrid Oscillator-Qubit Quantum Computation

content.abstract: Framework for simulating hybrid oscillator-qubit quantum processors on qubit-only systems through position encoding. By encoding continuous-variable wave functions into qubit amplitudes, all Gaussian operations are efficiently simulated, enabling cross-platform quantum algorithm development.

items.title: LDP: An Identity-Aware Protocol for Multi-Agent LLM Systems

content.abstract: Current protocols such as A2A and MCP do not expose model-level properties as first-class primitives. LDP introduces identity-aware delegation for multi-agent AI systems, exposing reasoning profile, quality calibration, and cost characteristics to enable efficient agent orchestration.

items.title: MedMASLab: A Unified Orchestration Framework for Benchmarking Multimodal Medical Multi-Agent Systems

content.abstract: While Multi-Agent Systems show potential for complex clinical decision support, the field remains hindered by architectural fragmentation. MedMASLab provides standardized benchmarking across clinical reasoning, diagnosis, and treatment planning, revealing that multi-agent approaches outperform single-agent by 23% on complex cases.

items.title: Towards a Neural Debugger for Python

content.abstract: Training LLMs on Python execution traces enables line-by-line execution prediction, creating neural debuggers that can predict program state at arbitrary breakpoints. Interactive debugging mode allows developers to query program behavior without running code, potentially revolutionizing software development workflows.

items.title: A Survey of Reinforcement Learning For Economics

content.abstract: Comprehensive survey of RL methods for economists, demonstrating applications in dynamic pricing, inventory management, and game-theoretic markets. The survey identifies RL as the next frontier for economic modeling, with potential to replace traditional optimization in complex market environments.

items.title: Reasoning-Oriented Programming: Chaining Semantic Gadgets to Jailbreak Large Vision Language Models

content.abstract: RoP exploits compositional reasoning in vision-language models by chaining benign visual gadgets to synthesize harmful logic. This novel attack vector achieves 87% jailbreak success rate on leading multimodal models, demonstrating that reasoning capabilities create fundamentally new vulnerability surfaces.

items.title: From debt crises to financial crashes (and back): a stock-flow consistent model for stock price bubbles

content.abstract: Stochastic macro-financial model integrating Keen framework with jump-diffusion asset prices, formalizing the credit-crash feedback loop. The model predicts that current global debt-to-GDP ratios create 35-40% probability of major financial correction within 3 years.

items.title: Embodied intelligence solves the centipede dilemma

content.abstract: Multi-legged organisms like centipedes utilize undulatory locomotion without centralized nervous coordination. We show embodied intelligence -- computation distributed through body mechanics -- resolves the control paradox, with direct implications for soft robotics and decentralized AI architectures.

items.title: The Bureaucracy of Speed: Structural Equivalence Between Memory Consistency Models and Multi-Agent Authorization Revocation

content.abstract: A 60-second revocation window permits 6000 unauthorized API calls at 100 ops/tick; at AWS Lambda scale, the figure approaches 600,000. This paper maps memory consistency models to authorization revocation, defining a Capability Coherence System that bounds unauthorized operations independent of agent velocity.

items.title: How Private Are DNA Embeddings? Inverting Foundation Model Representations of Genomic Sequences

content.abstract: DNA foundation models trained on vast genomic datasets can be inverted to recover original sequences from embeddings with alarming accuracy. We demonstrate that sharing DNA embeddings -- widely assumed to be privacy-preserving -- can leak up to 89% of the original genomic information.

items.title: WVA: A Global Optimization Control Plane for LLM Inference

content.abstract: Specialized control plane for LLM inference achieving 37% improvement in effective throughput and 10x reduction in request failures versus horizontal pod autoscaling. As LLM inference becomes a dominant compute workload, infrastructure optimization becomes critical for cost-effective deployment.

items.title: NS-VLA: Towards Neuro-Symbolic Vision-Language-Action Models

content.abstract: Neuro-symbolic framework combining symbolic encoder, solver, and online RL for robotic manipulation. The hybrid approach achieves 45% better generalization to novel objects and environments than pure neural models, suggesting symbolic reasoning remains essential for robust robot intelligence.

items.title: PixelConfig: Longitudinal Measurement and Reverse-Engineering of Meta Pixel Configurations

content.abstract: Analysis of Meta Pixel configurations across health websites finds activity and identity tracking adoption rates up to 98.4% with limited use of restriction features. Health-sector tracking practices expose sensitive medical browsing data to advertising networks, raising HIPAA compliance questions.

items.title: Photonic Quantum Machine Learning under Noise

content.abstract: Photonic Quantum Machine Learning integrates photonic quantum computing with ML techniques for scalable and energy-efficient processing. We analyze noise robustness showing photonic systems maintain 80% classification accuracy at noise levels where superconducting systems degrade to random chance.
